# Issue 100: `logP` must have one source of truth — remove redundant definitions across packages

**Scope:** `packages/core/src/Molecule.ts`, `packages/pharmacology/src/Types.ts`, `packages/pharmacology/src/Drug.ts`, `packages/biochem/src/lipid/Lipid.ts`

**Problem:** `logP` is defined in multiple places with different types (number, branded Lipophilicity, abstract override).

**Current (fragmented):**
```ts
// core/src/Molecule.ts
abstract get logP(): number;

// pharmacology/src/Types.ts
export type Lipophilicity = number & { readonly __unit: "logP" };

// pharmacology/src/Drug.ts
abstract override get logP(): number;

// biochem/src/lipid/Lipid.ts
override get logP(): number { ... }
```

**Expected (one source of truth):**
```ts
// core/src/Molecule.ts — THE ONLY DEFINITION
abstract class Molecule {
  /**
   * Octanol-water partition coefficient — measures lipophilicity.
   * High LogP (> 0): lipophilic. Low LogP (< 0): hydrophilic.
   */
  abstract get logP(): number;
}

// All subclasses — just override
class Rifampicin extends Drug {
  override readonly logP = 3.7;
}

class FattyAcid extends Lipid {
  override readonly logP = 4.5;
}
```

**Actions:**
1. Remove `Lipophilicity` branded type from `pharmacology/src/Types.ts` — redundant with `Molecule.logP`
2. Remove `abstract override get logP()` from `pharmacology/src/Drug.ts` — already abstract in `Molecule`
3. Keep only `override get logP()` in concrete subclasses

**Reason:** Single source of truth ensures consistency. `logP` is a universal molecular property defined once in the base class, not re-declared in every package.
